# Salesforce Spend Profile

**Last updated:** 2024-10-15  
**Owner:** Finance / Revenue Systems  
**Classification:** Confidential

## Overview

Salesforce is used by Sales (CRM), Marketing (Marketing Cloud Account Engagement, formerly Pardot), and Customer Success (Service Cloud). Spend has grown 31% over the past two years, outpacing headcount growth — a concern raised by the CFO in Q2 planning.

## License Inventory

| Cloud | Edition | Seats | Annual Cost |
|-------|---------|-------|-------------|
| Sales Cloud | Enterprise | 185 | $351,500 |
| Service Cloud | Professional | 42 | $63,000 |
| Marketing Cloud Acct Engagement | Growth | 1 org | $50,000 |
| Salesforce CPQ | — | 55 | $82,500 |
| **Total** | | | **$547,000** |

## Key Issues

**CPQ underutilization:** CPQ was purchased 18 months ago. RevOps reports ~40% of licensed reps have completed onboarding. Finance is flagging this to RevOps leadership.

**Dueling integrations:** We're paying for both the Salesforce-Slack integration and a third-party connector (Zapier flows) that do overlapping things. IT to audit and consolidate.

## Renewal Timeline

- Sales Cloud + Service Cloud renew: 2025-07-01
- Marketing Cloud: 2025-11-01
- Begin vendor discussions no later than 2025-03-01

## Optimization Levers

1. Audit inactive Sales Cloud seats (target: identify 15–20 reclaim candidates)
2. Downgrade 30 Sales Cloud Enterprise to Professional where feature usage is minimal
3. Negotiate multi-cloud bundle discount (Salesforce typically offers 8–12% for multi-cloud commits)

## Notes

Salesforce AE is Marcus Webb. Historically responsive. Our account qualifies for Success Plan Premier — worth negotiating into the next contract at no incremental cost.
